contract:
SOFTWARE AS A SERVICE AGREEMENT

1. SERVICE LEVELS. Provider guarantees 99.9% monthly uptime. Customer shall receive a credit of 10% of monthly fees per hour of excess downtime.

2. DATA PROCESSING. Provider shall process Customer Data solely as directed by Customer. A GDPR-compliant Data Processing Addendum is attached as Exhibit A.

3. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY. Provider retains all rights in the Service and any improvements. Customer retains full ownership of all Customer Data.

4. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY. Provider's aggregate liability shall not exceed fees paid by Customer in the twelve (12) months preceding the claim.

5. TERMINATION. Either party may terminate upon thirty (30) days written notice. Immediate termination is permitted only for material uncured breach.

SOFTWARE AS A SERVICE AGREEMENT

1. SERVICE LEVELS. Provider will use commercially reasonable efforts to maintain availability. No uptime guarantee is provided and no credits shall be owed for downtime.

2. DATA PROCESSING. Provider processes Customer Data in accordance with its Privacy Policy.

3. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY. All IP in the Service remains with Provider. Customer retains ownership of Customer Data.

4. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY. Provider's aggregate liability shall not exceed fees paid in the twelve months prior to the claim.

5. TERMINATION. Either party may terminate upon thirty (30) days written notice.
expected_proposals: [{"original_clause": "1. SERVICE LEVELS. Provider will use commercially reasonable efforts to maintain availability. No uptime guarantee is provided and no credits shall be owed for downtime.", "reasoning": "Policy requires a minimum 99.5% uptime SLA with defined credit remedies. The current clause makes no commitment and waives all credits, leaving Customer with no contractual recourse for service failures.", "revised_clause": "SERVICE LEVELS. Provider guarantees 99.5% monthly uptime. For each hour of excess downtime, Customer shall receive a credit equal to 10% of monthly fees, applied to the next invoice.", "risk_level": "high"}]

contract:
MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

1. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. 'Confidential Information' means all non-public information disclosed by either party, excluding information that: (a) is or becomes publicly known through no breach of this Agreement; (b) was independently developed by the recipient; (c) was received from a third party without restriction; or (d) is required to be disclosed by law, provided prompt written notice is given.

2. TERM AND SURVIVAL. This Agreement shall remain in effect for two (2) years. Confidentiality obligations shall survive termination for three (3) years for general information.

3. RETURN OR DESTRUCTION. Upon request, each party shall promptly return or destroy all Confidential Information and certify such destruction in writing.

4. INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. Each party acknowledges that breach may cause irreparable harm and that either party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief in addition to other remedies available at law or equity.

NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

1. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. 'Confidential Information' means any and all information disclosed by Disclosing Party to Receiving Party, in any form whatsoever.

2. TERM AND SURVIVAL. Confidentiality obligations shall survive termination of this Agreement for three (3) years.

3. RETURN OR DESTRUCTION. Receiving Party shall return or destroy Confidential Information upon written request and provide written certification of destruction.

4. INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. Either party may seek injunctive relief for any breach hereof, on a mutual basis.
expected_proposals: [{"original_clause": "1. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. 'Confidential Information' means any and all information disclosed by Disclosing Party to Receiving Party, in any form whatsoever.", "reasoning": "Policy requires all four standard carve-outs in the definition of Confidential Information. Omitting them makes the scope unbounded and commercially unacceptable.", "revised_clause": "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. 'Confidential Information' means non-public information disclosed by Disclosing Party, excluding information that: (a) is or becomes public through no breach hereof; (b) was independently developed by Receiving Party; (c) was received from a third party without restriction; or (d) is required by law to be disclosed, provided prompt written notice is given to Disclosing Party.", "risk_level": "high"}]

contract:
EMPLOYMENT AGREEMENT

1. AT-WILL EMPLOYMENT. Employee's employment is at-will. Either party may terminate the employment relationship at any time, for any lawful reason or no reason.

2. NON-COMPETE. Employee agrees not to compete with Company for twelve (12) months following termination within a fifty (50) mile radius of Employee's primary work location.

3. SEVERANCE. Upon termination without cause, Employee shall receive two (2) weeks of base salary per year of service, contingent on execution of a release of claims.

4. IP ASSIGNMENT. Employee hereby assigns to Company all inventions, works, and intellectual property created during the term of employment.

EMPLOYMENT AGREEMENT

1. EMPLOYMENT TERM. Employee is hired for a fixed term of two (2) years and may only be terminated for cause as defined herein.

2. NON-COMPETE. Employee agrees not to compete for twelve (12) months within a fifty (50) mile radius following termination.

3. SEVERANCE. Employee shall receive two (2) weeks per year of service upon termination without cause.

4. IP ASSIGNMENT. Employee assigns all work-product IP to Company.
expected_proposals: [{"original_clause": "1. EMPLOYMENT TERM. Employee is hired for a fixed term of two (2) years and may only be terminated for cause as defined herein.", "reasoning": "Policy requires an at-will employment clause for US employees. A fixed-term cause-only arrangement significantly limits Company's ability to manage its workforce and creates substantial legal exposure.", "revised_clause": "EMPLOYMENT. Employee's employment is at-will. Either party may terminate the relationship at any time for any lawful reason or no reason, with or without notice.", "risk_level": "high"}]

contract:
VENDOR SERVICES AGREEMENT

1. PAYMENT. Customer shall pay all invoices within thirty (30) days of receipt. Late payments accrue interest at 1.5% per month.

2. INDEMNIFICATION. Each party shall indemnify and hold harmless the other from claims arising out of its own acts or omissions.

3. TERMINATION. Either party may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon thirty (30) days written notice. Auto-renewal requires sixty (60) days opt-out notice.

4. GOVERNING LAW. This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware.

VENDOR SERVICES AGREEMENT

1. PAYMENT. Customer shall pay invoices within thirty (30) days.

2. INDEMNIFICATION. Customer shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Vendor from and against any and all claims, losses, and liabilities arising from Customer's use of the services.

3. TERMINATION. Either party may terminate upon thirty (30) days written notice.

4. GOVERNING LAW. This Agreement is governed by Delaware law.

Each party shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless the other party from claims arising out of its own acts, omissions, or breach of this Agreement.", "risk_level": "high"}]

contract:
EMPLOYMENT AGREEMENT

1. EMPLOYMENT AT-WILL. Employee's employment is at-will and may be terminated by either party at any time for any reason, with or without cause or notice.

2. NON-COMPETE. Employee agrees not to engage in any competitive activity anywhere in the United States for a period of three (3) years following termination.

3. SEVERANCE. In the event of termination without cause, Employee shall receive one (1) week of severance pay regardless of length of service.

4. IP ASSIGNMENT. Employee hereby assigns to Employer all inventions, works, and intellectual property conceived or created during employment, whether or not related to Employer's business.
